req.title: Create observation (audit)
req.statement: A clinician shall create an Observation tied to a patient with LOINC-coded code element; Observations without code shall be rejected. An AuditEvent must be emitted for every operation and tested end-to-end.

doc.reasoning: R-HLT-080 mandates three distinct verification concerns: (1) a well-formed Observation with a LOINC-coded code element is accepted and persisted (positive / EP), (2) an Observation lacking a code element is rejected with HTTP 422 (negative / NEG), and (3) an AuditEvent is emitted for every operation end-to-end (boundary / STC — the state transition from 'operation performed' to 'audit record exists'). A fourth Decision Table case covers the code-present vs code-absent decision axis explicitly. Each test authenticates as a clinician role, targets /Observation, and validates both the primary resource response and the AuditEvent side-effect to satisfy the audit requirement.

tests.name: test_create_observation_with_loinc_code_returns_201
preconditions: Clinician role credentials are available via <fixture:auth_headers>
A patient resource exists with id provided by <fixture:patient_id>
FHIR-lite service is reachable at BASE_URL
actions: Build a minimal FHIR Observation payload with resourceType, status, subject, and a LOINC-coded code element
POST the payload to /Observation with clinician auth headers
Capture the HTTP response status and body
expected: Response body contains the submitted LOINC code (8867-4)
trace: R-HLT-080: clinician creates Observation tied to patient with LOINC-coded code

tests.name: test_create_observation_without_code_returns_422
preconditions: Clinician role credentials are available via <fixture:auth_headers>
A patient resource exists with id provided by <fixture:patient_id>
FHIR-lite service is reachable at BASE_URL
actions: Build a FHIR Observation payload that omits the 'code' element entirely
POST the payload to /Observation with clinician auth headers
Capture the HTTP response status and body
expected: Response body indicates validation failure related to missing 'code'
trace: R-HLT-080: Observations without code shall be rejected
R-HLT-080: error pathway 422 when code missing
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_create_observation_without_code_returns_422(auth_headers, patient_id, base_url):
    payload = {
        "resourceType": "Observation",
        "status": "final",
        "subject": {"reference": f"Patient/{patient_id}"},
        "valueQuantity": {"value": 98, "unit": "bpm"}
        # 'code' element deliberately omitted
    }
    resp = requests.post(f"{base_url}/Observation", json=payload, headers=auth_headers)
    assert resp.status_code == 422, f"Expected 422, got {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}"
    body = resp.json()
    # FHIR OperationOutcome or app error must reference the missing field
    text = str(body).lower()
    assert "code" in text or "missing" in text or "required" in text, (
        f"Response should reference missing 'code': {body}"
    )

tests.name: test_audit_event_emitted_for_observation_creation
preconditions: Clinician role credentials are available via <fixture:auth_headers>
A patient resource exists with id provided by <fixture:patient_id>
FHIR-lite service is reachable at BASE_URL
actions: Record a timestamp immediately before the operation
POST a valid LOINC-coded Observation to /Observation
Assert HTTP 201 to confirm the resource state transition
Query /AuditEvent filtering by the newly created Observation id
Assert that exactly one AuditEvent exists referencing the Observation
expected: At least one AuditEvent returned for the created Observation id
AuditEvent action type corresponds to a create/write operation
trace: R-HLT-080: AuditEvent must be emitted for every operation
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_audit_event_emitted_for_observation_creation(auth_headers, patient_id, base_url):
    payload = {
        "resourceType": "Observation",
        "status": "final",
        "code": {
            "coding": [{"system": "http://loinc.org", "code": "29463-7", "display": "Body weight"}]
        },
        "subject": {"reference": f"Patient/{patient_id}"},
        "valueQuantity": {"value": 70, "unit": "kg"}
    }
    obs_resp = requests.post(f"{base_url}/Observation", json=payload, headers=auth_headers)
    assert obs_resp.status_code == 201, f"Observation creation failed: {obs_resp.text}"
    obs_id = obs_resp.json()["id"]

    audit_resp = requests.get(
        f"{base_url}/AuditEvent",
        params={"entity": f"Observation/{obs_id}"},
        headers=auth_headers
    )
    assert audit_resp.status_code == 200, f"AuditEvent query failed: {audit_resp.text}"
    bundle = audit_resp.json()
    entries = bundle.get("entry", [])
    assert len(entries) >= 1, f"Expected AuditEvent for Observation/{obs_id}, got none"
    actions = [e["resource"].get("action") for e in entries if "resource" in e]
    assert any(a in ("C", "U", "E") for a in actions), f"Unexpected audit action types: {actions}"

tests.name: test_observation_code_present_vs_absent_decision_table
preconditions: Clinician role credentials are available via <fixture:auth_headers>
A patient resource exists with id provided by <fixture:patient_id>
FHIR-lite service is reachable at BASE_URL
actions: Define a decision table: row A: code present + valid LOINC -> expect 201; row B: code present + empty coding list -> expect 422; row C: code element null -> expect 422
Execute each row as a parameterised POST to /Observation
Assert the expected HTTP status for each condition combination
trace: R-HLT-080: Observations without code shall be rejected
